sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, a frequent water skier, sustained a knee injury while skiing on Lake Martin five days ago, describing a twisting mechanism of injury and immediate swelling.
He reports pain, swelling, and decreased range of motion in the right knee, with pain to palpation and tenderness on the medial aspect of the knee.
He has been managing his pain with over-the-counter Aleve and cold compresses.
Past medical history: The patient had a tonsillectomy in the past.
He is currently taking lisinopril 20mg daily for hypertension, which he reports is well-tolerated.
He has a history of high blood pressure, with home blood pressure readings of 125/80 mmHg.
Review of systems: The patient denies any numbness or tingling in his foot.
Current medications: Lisinopril 20mg daily.
Objective:
Vital signs: Blood pressure 120/77 mmHg.
Physical exam: The patient has ecchymosis and edema on the right knee, with an effusion present.
There is pain to palpation of the right medial knee, and the patient reports decreased range of motion with a negative varus and valgus test.
The patient has a palpable dorsalis pedis and posterior tibial pulse, with no lower extremity edema.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has a medial collateral ligament strain in the right knee.
Problem list: Medial collateral ligament strain; Hypertension; Past medical history of tonsillectomy.
Plan:
I prescribed meloxicam 15mg once daily to help manage pain and reduce swelling.
I ordered an x-ray of the right knee to rule out any fractures.
I referred the patient to physical therapy to strengthen the muscles around the knee and prevent further injury.
I also ordered a lipid panel to check the patient's lipid levels.
I administered a tetanus shot for health maintenance.
I advised the patient to rest the knee and avoid further skiing until the results of the x-ray are available.
Follow-up: The patient will return for follow-up once the x-ray results are available.
